metadata.name: Traefik BasicAuth Middleware
metadata.description: Traefik Middleware CRD for HTTP basic authentication using an htpasswd-formatted Kubernetes Secret.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Creates a Traefik Middleware resource for `basicAuth` so dashboards, internal tools, or staging routes can require simple username and password authentication.

## Prerequisites
- Traefik must be installed with the Kubernetes CRD provider enabled.
- Create the referenced Kubernetes Secret with htpasswd-formatted credentials before attaching the middleware to a route.

## Apply
- Render `main.yaml`, review the secret name and header behavior, then apply it with `kubectl apply -f main.yaml`.
- Attach the middleware to a test route and confirm the browser challenge and upstream headers behave the way you expect.

## Follow-Up
- Rotate the Secret contents with your normal secret-management workflow instead of editing the middleware.
- Use stronger identity layers for high-risk public services and reserve basic auth for lightweight protection or transitional setups.

## References
- [BasicAuth Middleware](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/middlewares/http/basicauth/)
- [Traefik Kubernetes CRD](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/routing/providers/kubernetes-crd/)

items.name: resource_name
items.title: Middleware name
items.description: Name to use for the Middleware resource.

items.name: realm
items.title: Realm
items.description: Realm string presented in the browser authentication prompt.

items.name: remove_header
items.title: Remove Authorization header
items.description: Strip the Authorization header before forwarding the request upstream.

items.name: header_field_enabled
items.title: Header field
items.description: Write the authenticated username into a custom request header.

items.name: header_field
items.title: Header field name
items.description: Request header name used for the authenticated username.
